title: Aureline graph confidence-rollup contract
description: Boundary schema for the deterministic confidence-rollup vocabulary every graph-backed surface (graph overlay, topology map, impact explorer, cited-explainer overlay, AI context assembler, review pack, support bundle, public query-family surface) reads when it summarises multi-source confidence into one label. This schema is a strict superset of the inline confidence_rollup slot defined on schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json: every workspace-graph node and edge MAY carry a confidence_rollup from the seed; this contract pins the rollup algorithm, conflict vocabulary, partial-workset visibility interaction, and per-surface rollup classes (node / edge / impact_reason / explainer_citation) so topology, impact, and explainer surfaces produce identical labels for the same inputs. Consumers MUST quote rolled_up_level verbatim and MUST NOT silently upgrade. Adding a new conflict_state, new partial_visibility_interaction, new floor_reason, or new surface_class is additive-minor and bumps confidence_rollup_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

confidence_rollup_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the confidence-rollup vocabulary. Current value is 1.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque equality-comparable string.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles. Never carries raw source bodies or raw URLs.

confidence_level.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#confidence_level. Consumers render the label verbatim; surfaces MUST NOT silently upgrade a low-confidence relation.

edge_evidence_state.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#edge_evidence_state. The rollup reads this to apply the evidence-state floor.

workset_visibility_state.description: Re-export of schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#workset_scope_ref.visibility. The rollup reads this to apply the partial-visibility floor.

surface_class.description: Which surface family this rollup serves. The same algorithm applies across all four classes; the surface_class is recorded so a downstream consumer can dispatch on it without re-deriving from the parent record.

contributor_kind.description: Vocabulary for the producer family that contributed this confidence value. Mirrors schemas/graph/workspace_graph_seed.schema.json#source_class plus a primary_producer / projecting_surface / cross_reference_walker / heuristic_inference split so the rollup record names the contributor without re-deriving it.

contributor_ref.description: Optional opaque ref to the contributor's stamp_id or producer_ref. Support bundles cite this verbatim.

evidence_state.description: Optional evidence-state observed by this contributor. Edges always carry one; node contributors typically omit.

weight_hint.description: Optional advisory weight hint for downstream UX. The rollup algorithm itself ignores weight_hint and applies the closed floor rules; weight_hint is renderer-only.

source_confidence_contributor.description: One per-contributor confidence input. Multi-source nodes and edges list one contributor per producer that asserted the relation.

conflict_state.description: Frozen conflict-state vocabulary. Names the family of disagreement the contributors expressed so consumers render a typed conflict chip rather than collapsing into a generic 'mixed' label. no_conflict: every contributor agrees within one level. level_split_high_and_low: contributors disagree by ≥ 2 levels (high + low). level_split_with_unknown: at least one contributor reported unknown. evidence_state_split_direct_and_inferred: at least one direct_evidence contributor and at least one inferred_relation contributor. evidence_state_split_direct_and_stale: direct_evidence vs stale_relation. evidence_state_split_direct_and_missing_anchor: direct_evidence vs missing_anchor. imported_vs_local_split: imported_evidence contributor and a local-authoritative contributor disagree. ai_vs_authoritative_split: ai_inference contributor and an authoritative_producer contributor disagree. partial_visibility_pulls_down: contributors agreed but the workset visibility (partial_visible / policy_hidden / missing_in_scope) pulled the rollup down.

floor_reason.description: Frozen floor-reason vocabulary. Names which closed rule produced the rolled_up_level so a reviewer can replay the rollup deterministically. The rollup algorithm applies floors in the listed order and records the most-restrictive floor in floor_reason.

scope_ref.description: Optional opaque scope ref the visibility applies to. Re-exports schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json scope ids.

hidden_member_count.description: Optional hidden member count when visibility_state == policy_hidden. Surfaces render the count badge; the exact hidden list never projects.

partial_visibility_interaction.description: How partial workset visibility interacted with this rollup. fully_visible has no effect. partial_visible / policy_hidden cap the rollup at medium. missing_in_scope pulls the rollup to low and pairs with a missing-anchor badge on the parent record.

anchor_drift_present.description: True when at least one source_anchor on the parent record carries a drift_state other than anchor_present_no_drift.

drift_state_count.description: Optional count of source_anchors carrying drift. Surfaces render an aggregate drift chip when count > 0.

worst_drift_state.description: The worst drift_state observed across the parent record's anchors. Re-exports schemas/graph/provenance_stamp.schema.json#source_anchor_drift_state.

anchor_drift_summary.description: Aggregate anchor-drift summary the rollup reads when it applies the anchor_drift floor. Drift on at least one anchor pulls the rollup to low.

rollup_id.description: Stable rollup id. Surfaces that quote the rollup (audit packets, support bundles, AI evidence packets) reuse this id rather than re-stating the contents.

subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the subject node_id, edge_id, impact_reason_slot id, or explainer_citation_slot id.

rolled_up_level.description: The single confidence label every surface renders verbatim. Computed deterministically from source_confidences, evidence_states, partial_visibility_interaction, and anchor_drift_summary by the algorithm spelled out in /docs/graph/provenance_and_confidence_contract.md.

source_confidences.description: Per-contributor confidence list the rollup summarises. minItems 1: a rollup with no contributors is a contract violation; surfaces fall back to the parent record's confidence_level instead.

evidence_states_present.description: Distinct evidence-states observed across edge contributors. Empty for node rollups.

rollup_note.description: Redaction-aware short note (≤ 240 graphemes) the surface renders verbatim alongside the rolled_up_level chip. Never embeds raw source bodies.

supporting_stamp_refs.description: Zero or more provenance_stamp_record stamp_ids that contributed to this rollup. Support bundles walk this list to assemble a forensic packet.

confidence_rollup_record.description: One confidence-rollup record. Multi-source nodes, edges, impact-reason slots, and explainer-citation slots resolve to one rollup per surface so graph UX, AI explainers, topology maps, impact explorers, and support bundles agree on one confidence label rather than cherry-picking a contributor.
